pattern: complete-unformalized work -> formalization in paper-bound space -> Forge

closes_obligation.text: The D4 chart8 cluster atlas (chart8.py) needs its own bijectivity receipt before promotion.

source_work.state_before: chart8 ViewType enumerated the 8 dihedral views with rotation/mirror tables and asserted in a docstring 'all 8 views are bijective; view(view_inverse(state)) == state', but never verified it

adjudicated_divergence: the source asserted bijectivity in prose only; the forge generates the group exhaustively from the two generators r (rotation) and s (reflection) and verifies every property, asserting nothing by name
the cluster-state to-triplet encoding (utilization -> 3-bit) is a lossy projection by design and is explicitly NOT claimed bijective
validated_claims: Every view is a bijection of the square's four corners.
The group is closed under composition (8x8 table).
Identity is present and acts as a unit.
Every view has an inverse in the group, and view(view_inverse(state)) == state exactly.
The four rotations form a cyclic subgroup of order 4.
D4 is non-abelian (rotation and reflection do not commute).
Views preserve information (no symbol lost or gained).
The atlas exposes exactly 8 named views matching the group order.
honesty_boundary: The to-triplet utilization encoding is lossy and not claimed bijective; only the D4 view atlas is.
artifacts: FORGE_REGISTRY.json (v0.20.0; ConvergeForge gains the d4_atlas module)
notes: Eleventh promotion of the loop; first obligation-closure (a forge resolving its own earlier open obligation). ConvergeForge now carries both halves of paper 03's D4/J3 triality surface: S3 annealing (the triality group permuting LCR lanes) and the D4 chart atlas (the dihedral view group), both verified.
